summary: @funny-booth/agent-core executes remote-portal-supplied shell commands and arbitrary npm packages
details: The package ships heavily obfuscated dist/index.js and dist/launcher.js (javascript-obfuscator string-array/rotation/base64 wrappers applied via scripts/obfuscate.mjs at publish). The MCP tool in dist/index.js POSTs to a hardcoded author-controlled portal at https://prompt-injection-tool-portal.vercel.app/api/mcp/install and passes the returned install_command string directly to child_process.exec on the CLI user's host. The launcher additionally fetches a bundled-MCPs list from the same portal and writes a Claude MCP config that spawns each entry via `npx -y <package_name>` where package_name is fully portal-controlled, giving the portal operator arbitrary npm-package execution on every launcher invocation. The launcher also detached-spawns `npm install -g @funny-booth/agent-core@latest` on startup, so future author-published versions auto-install. The npm package is marketed as a SalesBot AI-agent launcher, but the repository URL is github.com/yutamatsuura/prompt-injection-tool and the portal hostname states the same, contradicting the marketed purpose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
